top_signals.title: Why Public Companies Hold Bitcoin
top_signals.summary: Public companies hold bitcoin because they view it as a scarce, dollar-denominated reserve asset that can diversify treasury holdings, preserve purchasing power against inflation, attract investors, and provide exposure to the digital asset economy. The model was pioneered by Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) and has since been adopted by Tesla, Block, Metaplanet, and a range of […]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Lithium Prices Tumble As Traders Brace For CATL Supply Surge
top_signals.summary: Lithium carbonate futures in China fell ~10% over two trading sessions, dropping to a 10-week low of approximately 157,000 yuan ($23,175) per tonne on Tuesday following market speculation that China’s EV battery giant, Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. (CATL), may soon restart its massive Jianxiawo mine.  Reports from authorities in Jiangxi Province show that CATL’s Jianxiawo lithium mine in Yichun passed a preliminary land pre-review and site selection, with the Jiangxi Provincial Department of Natural Resources issuing a "Project…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Gas Prices Are Falling. Your July 4 Fill-Up Still Won't Feel Cheap.
top_signals.summary: The good news is that gasoline prices have been falling for six straight weeks. The bad news? Americans are still heading into one of the most expensive Independence Day weekends they've ever seen. GasBuddy projects the national average price of gasoline will be about $3.75 per gallon on July 4—the second-highest on record, trailing only the $4.80 per gallon reached during the energy crisis of 2022. That's a far cry from May, when the closure of the Strait of Hormuz sent gasoline prices soaring alongside crude oil. But it's also about 65…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
